question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setRoleSetter] Can an unprivileged caller (attacker != roleSetter) under the precondition that roleSetter is set to a non-zero address call setRoleSetter(attacker) directly, bypassing the require(msg.sender == roleSetter) guard, violating the invariant that only the current roleSetter can reassign the roleSetter role, causing scoped impact: unauthorized role takeover enabling downstream feeSetter/feeClaimer/tickSpacingSetter reassignment? [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + setRoleSetter] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that multicall uses delegatecall (preserving msg.sender) craft a multicall payload that includes setRoleSetter(attacker) as one of the calls, violating the invariant that only the current roleSetter can change the roleSetter, causing scoped impact: unauthorized role setter takeover? [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + setRoleSetter ordering] Can the current roleSetter under the precondition of a multicall with [setRoleSetter(newAddr), setFeeSetter(x)] cause the second call to fail because roleSetter was already transferred in the first call, violating the invariant that multicall atomically applies all role changes under the original caller's authority, causing scoped impact: fund freeze via inability to set feeSetter after roleSetter transfer in same multicall?
